author: fable-5
summary: Conducted full security review of daax-web (host-dev + Tailscale/Pocket ID). Wrote fable-revew.md.
key_findings: No default-deny authz: 89/133 routes lack requireAuth; 3 unauth RCE sinks (mcp/tools, plugins/mcp-inspector, code-server)
HTTP LOCAL_OPERATOR bypass has no loopback/peer check (unlike WS) -> 0.0.0.0 tailnet deploys expose operator
App container runs as root with docker.sock mounted -> any RCE => host root
DAAX_PROXY_SECRET not wired into app service in compose; strict mode would fail-closed/brick login
framing: Perimeter design (Traefik header-strip, HMAC WS tickets, fail-closed strict) is sound; issue is defense-in-depth failure behind it, exploitable via host-dev CSRF, sibling-container on daax-net (untrusted AI agent code), and proxy-less 0.0.0.0 exposure.
primary_fix: Single default-deny middleware.ts (auth + Origin/CSRF check) preserving loopback LOCAL_OPERATOR bypass = low friction.
validation: Producer Fable 5; advisor (stronger reviewer) reframed front-door-bypass -> defense-in-depth and surfaced host-dev CSRF vector, incorporated. Cross-provider (Codex) unavailable per repo memory.

author: fable-5
summary: Created 23 GitHub issues (daax-dev/daax-web #181-#203) from fable-revew.md, each labeled fable-review + security, with full context, exploitation scenario, fix scope, acceptance criteria, and required unit/integration/E2E test coverage per CLAUDE.md Definition of Done.
root_cause_issue: #181 (default-deny middleware.ts) is the primary architectural fix; most Critical/Medium unauth findings reference it as a dependency alongside their own route-specific fix.
